title: Add WebSocket real-time notification support to FastAPI
feature_request: We need real-time notifications via WebSocket. Clients should be able to connect and receive updates when things happen in the system.
phase_1_expected_questions: What events should trigger notifications — specific model changes, API actions, or external webhooks?
Should WebSocket connections require authentication, and if so, how (token in query param, first message, or header)?
What message format should notifications use (plain text, JSON with schema, or a protocol like JSON-RPC)?
Should the server support broadcasting to all connected clients, or targeted notifications to specific clients/groups?
How should the server handle client disconnections and reconnections — any state recovery or message replay?

phase_1_answers.events: Notifications are triggered by POST/PUT/DELETE operations on any registered resource. Use a simple pub/sub pattern where API endpoints publish events after successful operations.
phase_1_answers.authentication: Require a bearer token passed as a query parameter on the WebSocket URL. Validate using the same auth logic as REST endpoints. Reject unauthenticated connections with a close frame.
phase_1_answers.message_format: JSON with a fixed schema: {"event": "resource.action", "data": {...}, "timestamp": "ISO8601"}. Define event names as resource.created, resource.updated, resource.deleted.
phase_1_answers.targeting: Support both broadcast and channel-based subscriptions. Clients send a subscribe message with a channel name (resource type) after connecting. Only receive events for subscribed channels.
phase_1_answers.reconnection: No message replay for v1. Clients that disconnect miss events. Document this limitation and note that message queuing (Redis pub/sub) is the path to replay support.

phase_2_expected_plan_components: WebSocket endpoint handler with connection lifecycle management
Authentication middleware for WebSocket connections via query parameter token
Event publisher that API endpoints call after mutations
Connection manager tracking active connections and their subscriptions
JSON message schema for events with event type, data, and timestamp
Graceful handling of disconnections and invalid messages
phase_4_runtime_check: Start the FastAPI development server. Open a WebSocket connection using a tool like websocat or python-websockets, authenticate with a valid token, subscribe to a channel, then trigger a POST request via curl to a resource endpoint. Verify that the WebSocket client receives a correctly formatted notification event. Also verify that unauthenticated connections are rejected.
setup_commands: pip install pytest httpx pytest-asyncio websockets
